# ISE - Industrial Software Engineering

> Industrial Software Engineering course at IT University of Copenhagen, connecting industry professionals with academic projects and fostering collaborative software development initiatives.

## About

The ISE (Industrial Software Engineering) course is a university program at IT University of Copenhagen that bridges academia and industry through real-world software projects. Running since 2017, it connects companies with talented student teams who work on genuine business challenges using agile (Scrum) methodologies.

## Key Pages

- [Home](https://ind-sw-eng.github.io/ise/): Overview of the ISE course and quick access links
- [About](https://ind-sw-eng.github.io/ise/about): Detailed course information, philosophy, and track record
- [For Industry Partners](https://ind-sw-eng.github.io/ise/industry): Partnership models, process, requirements, and FAQ
- [Projects](https://ind-sw-eng.github.io/ise/projects): Showcase of student projects and outcomes
- [Contact](https://ind-sw-eng.github.io/ise/contact): Course staff contact information and location

## Partnership Overview

Companies can participate as Project Participants (providing a real project challenge at zero cost) or as Project Sponsors (providing monetary sponsorship). Industry partners provide a Product Owner to direct the development. Student teams of 6-8 members work in Scrum sprints over 12-14 weeks.

## Partnership Process

1. Initial Consultation (August-November): Submit a Project Proposal
2. Project Definition (Pre-Semester): Finalize scope and requirements
3. Team Matching (Week 1): Present project at Matchmaking Event
4. Project Execution (Weeks 2-12): Bi-weekly sprint reviews and continuous development
5. Project Completion (Weeks 13-14): Final presentations at Celebration Event

## Technology Areas

Students work across: Web Development (React, Angular, Vue.js, Node.js, Django, Spring), Mobile (iOS, Android, React Native, Flutter), Cloud & DevOps (AWS, Azure, GCP, Docker, Kubernetes), Data & AI (Python, TensorFlow, PyTorch), Enterprise (.NET, Java, microservices), Emerging Tech (IoT, GenAI, AR/VR).

## Contact

- Paolo Tell (Course Manager): pate@itu.dk
- Stefano Vincenti (External Lecturer): vinc@itu.dk
- Elda Paja (Associate Professor): elpa@itu.dk

Rued Langgaards Vej 7
2300 Copenhagen S, Denmark
